title: Aleksanteri Institute students recognized for outstanding theses
content:
Aleksanteri Institute students recognized for outstanding theses

turmaija@helsinki.fi Fri, 29/05/2026 - 12:01
We warmly congratulate our students for success in the FAREES MA Thesis Competition! MAREEES student Sierra Salazar won the award for the best master’s thesis, and MAREEES student Tabea Böing received an honorable mention together with Essi Saborit from Tampere University, who is one of our ExpREES/VIExpert students.

title: New MSCA-funded project by Sabine Volk explores anti-gender mobilisation across Europe
content:
New MSCA-funded project by Sabine Volk explores anti-gender mobilisation across Europe

klecarrh@helsinki.fi Fri, 29/05/2026 - 10:53
HEPP is pleased to announce a new research project by HEPPster Sabine Volk. Funded through the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions framework (2026–2028), The Translocal Far Right in Europe: Exploring the Mobilisation Against “Gender” (REXGEN) investigates how far-right actors across Europe mobilise against gender equality..

content:

txrissan@helsinki.fi Fri, 29/05/2026 - 10:39
The Doctoral School of our university has announced the four recipients of the Excellence in Doctoral Supervision Award for 2026. Among them is Mari Tervaniemi, Co-head of CBRU and Research Director—our warmest congratulations to Mari!

title: New publication: Patient-derived cardiomyocytes reveal vulnerabilities in hypoplastic left heart syndrome
content:
New publication: Patient-derived cardiomyocytes reveal vulnerabilities in hypoplastic left heart syndrome

vaha@helsinki.fi Fri, 29/05/2026 - 07:17
Our new article led by Margarida Varela and carried out in collaboration with Emmi Helle’s group at the Faculty of Medicine provides new insights into the cellular mechanisms underlying hypoplastic left heart syndrome (HLHS), one of the most severe congenital heart diseases.

title: Looping Local Mushroom Operations in Viikki: A New Pilot Project Testing Urban Food Upcycling
content:
Looping Local Mushroom Operations in Viikki: A New Pilot Project Testing Urban Food Upcycling

tipelkon@helsinki.fi Thu, 28/05/2026 - 14:29
Can mushrooms show the way to more circular urban food system? That is explored in the Urban Mushroom Upcycling Operations Pilot covering cultivation, consumption, and composite creation from oyster mushrooms.

title: Ekologian, taloustieteen ja tilastotieteen yhdistävään tutkimukseen 2,5 miljoonaa euroa
content:
2.5 million euros awarded for research combining ecology, economics and statistics

pellijoh@helsinki.fi Thu, 28/05/2026 - 14:27
The Jane and Aatos Erkko Foundation has awarded 2.5 million euros to a University of Helsinki research project aimed at bringing natural capital into decision-making. The interdisciplinary project develops new ways to make the impacts of biodiversity visible in the economy and society.

title: RADIA on mukana NUORA-hankkeessa
content:
RADIA participates in the NUORA project

lepomiro@helsinki.fi Thu, 28/05/2026 - 10:38
RADIA is involved in the NUORA project, which aims to produce a multidimensional, research-based understanding of youth radicalization, gang formation, and recruitment into criminal activity and violent extremism in Finland

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

title: Tutkijat ja yritykset ratkoivat yhdessä koulutushaasteita Etelä-Afrikassa
content:
Researchers and business join forces to solve educational challenges in South Africa

hmustajo@helsinki.fi Fri, 29/05/2026 - 16:17
Finnish businesses are seeking a foothold on the African continent, where deficiencies in education can often hinder their success. A joint project between the University of Helsinki, South African higher education institutions and businesses developed new forms of education to solve the country’s skills shortage.
